title: Publications | Stats NZ
content:
## LEED quarterly technical notes
Technical notes about the quarterly statistics produced from the Linked Employer-Employee Data (LEED).

## Exogenous versus endogenous rates of return: The user cost of capital in Statistics New Zealand's multifactor productivity measures
Provides a summary of the work undertaken to date on productivity measurement in New Zealand.

## Seasonal employment patterns in the horticultural industry
This study uses Linked-Employer-Employee Data (LEED) to examine short-term and seasonal employment patterns within the New Zealand horticulture industry.

## Guide to interpreting the LEED Data
Introduces official quarterly statistics produced from the Linked Employer-Employee Data (LEED), which measure labour market dynamics.

## Linked Employer-Employee Data Project: Project Scope
This Linked Employer-Employee Data (LEED) project looks at business and employment performance and dynamics by utilising existing data sources.

## Pacific Progress: A Report on the Economic Status of Pacific Peoples in New Zealand
Provides information on the social and economic status of Pacific peoples in New Zealand, based on data from the 2001 Census of Population and Dwellings.

## A longitudinal look at some data of the Household Labour Force Survey
This project explores the potential of the Household Labour Force Survey (HLFS) data base for analysis of labour market flows.

## Gender and unpaid work findings from the Time Use Survey
Examines the differences in participation in unpaid work and how it varies for different groups, particularly between men and women.

## Investigation of the delete-a-group jackknife variance estimator for the Household Labour Force Survey
Investigates whether the delete-a-group jackknife variance estimator could be used to produce sample errors for the Household Labour Force Survey (HLFS).
